Core Modules¶

Configuration and constants¶

Configuration models and resolution helpers for SPADES runs.

class spades.config.DoubleBetaProcess(type, transition=TransitionTypes.ZEROPLUS_TO_ZEROPLUS, mechanism=NeutrinoLessModes.LIGHT_NEUTRINO_EXCHANGE)[source]¶

Bases: object

Physics process selection for a computation.

Parameters:

  • type (ProcessTypes) –

  • transition (TransitionTypes) –

  • mechanism (NeutrinoLessModes) –

type: ProcessTypes¶
transition: TransitionTypes = 1¶
mechanism: NeutrinoLessModes = 1¶
class spades.config.BoundConfig(max_r, n_radial_points, n_values, k_values)[source]¶

Bases: object

Configuration for bound-state Dirac wavefunctions.

Parameters:

  • max_r (float) –

  • n_radial_points (int) –

  • n_values (list[int]) –

  • k_values (dict[int, list[int]]) –

max_r: float¶
n_radial_points: int¶
n_values: list[int]¶
k_values: dict[int, list[int]]¶
class spades.config.ScatteringConfig(max_r, n_radial_points, min_ke, max_ke, n_ke_points, k_values)[source]¶

Bases: object

Configuration for scattering-state Dirac wavefunctions.

Parameters:

  • max_r (float) –

  • n_radial_points (int) –

  • min_ke (float) –

  • max_ke (float) –

  • n_ke_points (int) –

  • k_values (list[int]) –

max_r: float¶
n_radial_points: int¶
min_ke: float¶
max_ke: float¶
n_ke_points: int¶
k_values: list[int]¶
class spades.config.SpectraConfig(method, wave_function_evaluation, nuclear_radius, types, fermi_function_types, energy_grid_type, n_ke_points, min_ke, total_ke, ei_ef=-1.0, corrections=<factory>, compute_2d=False, n_points_log_2d=0, n_points_lin_2d=0, e_max_log_2d=-1.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Configuration used to compute spectra and phase-space factors.

Parameters:

  • method (dict) –

  • wave_function_evaluation (int) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • types (list[SpectrumTypes]) –

  • fermi_function_types (list[FermiFunctionTypes]) –

  • energy_grid_type (int) –

  • n_ke_points (int) –

  • min_ke (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • corrections (list[CorrectionTypes]) –

  • compute_2d (bool) –

  • n_points_log_2d (int) –

  • n_points_lin_2d (int) –

  • e_max_log_2d (float) –

method: dict¶
wave_function_evaluation: int¶
nuclear_radius: float¶
types: list[SpectrumTypes]¶
fermi_function_types: list[FermiFunctionTypes]¶
energy_grid_type: int¶
n_ke_points: int¶
min_ke: float¶
total_ke: float¶
ei_ef: float = -1.0¶
corrections: list[CorrectionTypes]¶
compute_2d: bool = False¶
n_points_log_2d: int = 0¶
n_points_lin_2d: int = 0¶
e_max_log_2d: float = -1.0¶
class spades.config.OutputConfig(location, what, file_prefix)[source]¶

Bases: object

Output selection and destination configuration.

Parameters:

  • location (str) –

  • what (list[str]) –

  • file_prefix (str) –

location: str¶
what: list[str]¶
file_prefix: str¶
solve_output_config()[source]¶

Convert textual output requests into boolean switches.

class spades.config.RunConfig(config)[source]¶

Bases: object

Runtime configuration wrapper with unit conversion and option resolution.

Parameters:

config (dict) –

resolve_nuclear_radius(initial_atom)[source]¶

Resolve nuclear radius from explicit input or the default 1.2 A^(1/3) model.

Parameters:

initial_atom (AtomicSystem) – Initial atomic system used to extract mass number A for the automatic model.

resolve_ei_ef(initial_atom, final_atom)[source]¶

Resolve available kinetic energy and nuclear level spacing for the selected process.

Parameters:

  • initial_atom (AtomicSystem) – Initial atomic system.

  • final_atom (AtomicSystem) – Final atomic system (reserved for future use in this routine).

resolve_bound_config(initial_atom)[source]¶

Populate missing bound-state quantum numbers from the atomic configuration.

Parameters:

initial_atom (AtomicSystem) – Atomic system providing shell quantum numbers for automatic configuration.

create_spectra_config()[source]¶

Build SpectraConfig and map string options to typed enums.

create_bound_config()[source]¶

Build BoundConfig when bound-state settings are provided.

create_scattering_config()[source]¶

Build ScatteringConfig with kinetic-energy bounds from spectra settings.

create_output_config()[source]¶

Build and finalize output options if an output block exists.

Physical constants, units, enums, and name maps used across SPADES.

spades.ph.read_mass_difference(file_name)[source]¶

Load tabulated mass differences from YAML.

Parameters:

file_name (str) – Relative file name inside data/mass_difference or an absolute path.

spades.ph.to_distance_units()[source]¶

Return conversion factor from internal fm to user_distance_unit.

class spades.ph.ProcessTypes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Supported double-beta decay channels.

TWONEUTRINO_TWOBMINUS = 1¶
NEUTRINOLESS_TWOBMINUS = 2¶
TWONEUTRINO_TWOBPLUS = 3¶
NEUTRINOLESS_TWOBPLUS = 4¶
TWONEUTRINO_BPLUSEC = 5¶
NEUTRINOLESS_BPLUSEC = 6¶
TWONEUTRINO_TWOEC = 7¶
class spades.ph.TransitionTypes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Nuclear transition channels between initial and final states.

ZEROPLUS_TO_ZEROPLUS = 1¶
ZEROPLUS_TO_ZEROTWOPLUS = 2¶
ZEROPLUS_TO_TWOPLUS = 3¶
class spades.ph.TaylorOrders(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Orders used in the Taylor-expansion spectra method.

ZERO = 0¶
TWO = 2¶
TWOTWO = 22¶
FOUR = 4¶
SIX = 6¶
class spades.ph.NeutrinoLessModes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Neutrinoless mechanisms supported by SPADES.

LIGHT_NEUTRINO_EXCHANGE = 1¶
class spades.ph.FermiFunctionTypes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Fermi-function evaluation backends.

POINTLIKE_FERMIFUNCTIONS = 1¶
CHARGEDSPHERE_FERMIFUNCTIONS = 2¶
NUMERIC_FERMIFUNCTIONS = 3¶
class spades.ph.SpectrumMethod(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Spectrum-computation methods.

CLOSUREMETHOD = 1¶
TAYLORMETHOD = 2¶
class spades.ph.SpectrumTypes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Output spectra families.

SINGLESPECTRUM = 1¶
SUMMEDSPECTRUM = 2¶
ANGULARSPECTRUM = 3¶
ALPHASPECTRUM = 4¶
class spades.ph.CorrectionTypes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Corrections that can be applied to spectra/PSFs.

EXCHANGE_CORRECTION = 1¶
RADIATIVE_CORRECTION = 2¶
class spades.ph.WFEvaluationTypes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Wavefunction evaluation strategies at the nuclear scale.

ONSURFACE = 1¶
WEIGHTED = 2¶
class spades.ph.OutputFormatTypes(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Supported output serialization formats.

JSONFORMAT = 1¶
HDF5FORMAT = 2¶

Wavefunctions and atomic structure¶

Atomic configuration models and DHFS wrapper orchestration.

class spades.dhfs.AtomicSystem(name='', atomic_number=-1, mass_number=-1, electron_config='auto', weight=-1.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Atomic nucleus plus electron-shell configuration used by DHFS and RADIAL.

print()[source]¶

Print a readable summary of the DHFS electron configuration.

net_charge()[source]¶

Return net ionic charge Z - N_e.

spades.dhfs.create_ion(atom, z_nuc)[source]¶

Clone an atomic system and update its nuclear charge to z_nuc.

Parameters:

atom (AtomicSystem) –

Return type:

AtomicSystem

class spades.dhfs.DHFSHandler(config, label, atomic_weight=-1.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Orchestrate DHFS runs and expose wavefunctions, potentials, and shell energies.

Parameters:

  • config (AtomicSystem) –

  • label (str) –

  • atomic_weight (float) –

print()[source]¶

Print handler metadata and underlying atomic configuration.

run_dhfs(max_radius, n_points=1000)[source]¶

Organizes the call to DHFS_MAIN from DHFS.f. Sets appropriate parameters first.

Args:

max_radius (float): Maximum radius for radial grid n_points (int, optional): Number of radial points to use (tentative). Defaults to 1000.

Raises:

ValueError: _description_

Parameters:

max_radius (float) –

retrieve_dhfs_results()[source]¶

Retrieve DHFS wavefunctions, potentials, and binding energies.

build_modified_potential()[source]¶

Builds the modified DHFS potential a la [Nitescu et al, Phys. Rev. C 107, 025501, 2023] The resulting potential is suitable for the computation of both bound and scattering states and yields scattering states orthogonal on bound ones.

Returns:

np.ndarray: r times the modified potential

Return type:

ndarray

Bound and scattering electron wavefunction solvers.

class spades.wavefunctions.BoundHandler(z_nuc, n_e, bound_states_configuration)[source]¶

Bases: object

Compute bound-state Dirac wavefunctions for a fixed central potential.

Parameters:

  • z_nuc (int) –

  • n_e (int) –

  • bound_states_configuration (BoundConfig) –

set_potential(r_grid, rv_grid)[source]¶

Load r*V(r) potential into the RADIAL backend.

Parameters:

  • r_grid (ndarray) – Radial grid in fm.

  • rv_grid (ndarray) – r * V(r) values in MeV*fm.

find_bound_states(binding_energies=None)[source]¶

Solve requested bound shells and build interpolation splines.

probability_in_sphere(radius, n, kappa)[source]¶

Evaluate shell probability density at radius radius.

Parameters:

  • radius (float) – Radius in fm where the density is evaluated.

  • n (int) – Principal quantum number.

  • kappa (int) – Relativistic angular quantum number.

Returns:

Probability-density-like factor used by capture-channel spectra.

Return type:

float

class spades.wavefunctions.ScatteringHandler(z_nuc, n_e, scattering_states_configuration)[source]¶

Bases: object

Compute continuum Dirac wavefunctions and phase shifts.

Parameters:

  • z_nuc (int) –

  • n_e (int) –

  • scattering_states_configuration (ScatteringConfig) –

set_potential(r_grid, rv_grid)[source]¶

Load potential and extract asymptotic charge for Coulomb phase shifts.

Parameters:

  • r_grid (ndarray) – Radial grid in fm.

  • rv_grid (ndarray) – r * V(r) values in MeV*fm.

compute_scattering_states()[source]¶

Solve the continuum equation for all configured kappa and energies.

class spades.wavefunctions.WaveFunctionsHandler(atom, bound_conf=None, scattering_conf=None, rad_grid=None, rv_grid=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

High-level orchestrator for DHFS, bound, and scattering wavefunctions.

Parameters:

  • atom (AtomicSystem) –

  • bound_conf (BoundConfig | None) –

  • scattering_conf (ScatteringConfig | None) –

  • rad_grid (ndarray | None) –

  • rv_grid (ndarray | None) –

run_dhfs_neutral_or_positive_ion()[source]¶

Run DHFS workflow for neutral or positively charged ions.

run_dhfs_negative_ion()[source]¶

Construct a modified potential for negative ions from reference systems.

run_dhfs()[source]¶

Dispatch DHFS handling based on net ionic charge.

Return type:

None

find_bound_states()[source]¶

Solve bound states if the process requires them.

find_scattering_states(r_grid_scattering=None, rv_scattering=None)[source]¶

Solve scattering states on the current potential.

Parameters:

  • r_grid_scattering (ndarray | None) – Reserved optional arguments for alternative scattering potentials.

  • rv_scattering (ndarray | None) – Reserved optional arguments for alternative scattering potentials.

find_all_wavefunctions()[source]¶

Run the complete bound/scattering workflow with DHFS fallback.

Corrections and helper modules¶

Fermi-function backends for Coulomb and finite-size corrections.

class spades.fermi_functions.FermiFunctions[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Abstract interface for evaluating Fermi-function components.

abstract ff0_eval(ke)[source]¶

Evaluate FF0 at kinetic energy ke.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

Return type:

float

abstract ff1_eval(ke)[source]¶

Evaluate FF1 at kinetic energy ke.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

Return type:

float

abstract ff_ecbeta_eval(ke)[source]¶

Evaluate EC-beta specific Fermi factor at kinetic energy ke.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

abstract g_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Evaluate complex/real g_kappa radial amplitude at kinetic energy ke.

Return type:

float

abstract f_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Evaluate complex/real f_kappa radial amplitude at kinetic energy ke.

Return type:

float

class spades.fermi_functions.PointLike(z, r, e_grid=None)[source]¶

Bases: FermiFunctions

Point-charge analytical Fermi-function model.

Parameters:

  • z (int) –

  • r (float) –

  • e_grid (ndarray | None) –

g_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Evaluate point-like g_kappa radial amplitude.

Parameters:

  • ke – Kinetic energy.

  • kappa – Relativistic angular quantum number.

Returns:

g_kappa amplitude.

Return type:

float

f_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Evaluate point-like f_kappa radial amplitude.

Parameters:

  • ke – Kinetic energy.

  • kappa – Relativistic angular quantum number.

Returns:

f_kappa amplitude.

Return type:

float

ff0_eval(ke)[source]¶

Return FF0(ke) from kappa=-1 and kappa=+1 amplitudes.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

ff1_eval(ke)[source]¶

Return FF1(ke) from kappa=-1 and kappa=+1 amplitudes.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

ff_ecbeta_eval(ke)[source]¶

Return EC-beta factor derived from point-like amplitudes.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

Return type:

float

class spades.fermi_functions.ChargedSphere(z, r)[source]¶

Bases: FermiFunctions

Approximate Fermi functions for a uniformly charged nuclear sphere.

Parameters:

  • z (int) –

  • r (float) –

ff0_eval(ke)[source]¶

Evaluate charged-sphere FF0 at kinetic energy ke.

ff1_eval(ke)[source]¶

Evaluate charged-sphere FF1 approximation at kinetic energy ke.

ff_ecbeta_eval(ke)[source]¶

EC-beta factor is not implemented for this approximation.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

Return type:

float

f_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Direct f_kappa amplitude is not implemented for this approximation.

g_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Direct g_kappa amplitude is not implemented for this approximation.

class spades.fermi_functions.Numeric(scattering_handler, radius, density_function=None)[source]¶

Bases: FermiFunctions

Fermi functions built from numerically computed scattering wavefunctions.

Parameters:

  • scattering_handler (ScatteringHandler) –

  • radius (float) –

  • density_function (Callable | None) –

ff0_eval(energy)[source]¶

Evaluate interpolated numeric FF0 at energy energy.

ff1_eval(energy)[source]¶

Evaluate interpolated numeric FF1 at energy energy.

ff_ecbeta_eval(ke)[source]¶

Evaluate interpolated numeric EC-beta factor.

Parameters:

ke (float) –

g_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Evaluate interpolated numeric g_kappa.

f_eval(ke, kappa)[source]¶

Evaluate interpolated numeric f_kappa.

build_fermi_functions()[source]¶

Assemble FF0 and FF1 splines from the kappa=±1 components.

Exchange-correction utilities built from bound/scattering wavefunction overlaps.

spades.exchange.integrate_wf(f1, f2, r1, r2)[source]¶

Integrate product of two radial wavefunction components on a common grid.

Parameters:

  • f1 (ndarray) –

  • f2 (ndarray) –

  • r1 (ndarray) –

  • r2 (ndarray) –

class spades.exchange.ExchangeCorrection(initial_handler, final_handler, nuclear_radius)[source]¶

Bases: object

Compute exchange-correction factors for beta-decay observables.

Parameters:

  • initial_handler (WaveFunctionsHandler) –

  • final_handler (WaveFunctionsHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

compute_overlaps(k)[source]¶

Compute scattering-bound and bound-bound overlaps for one kappa.

Parameters:

k (int) –

Return type:

tuple[ndarray, ndarray]

transform_scattering_wavefunctions()[source]¶

Transformation according to AIP Conf. Proc. 3138, 020012 (2024) https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0205270

compute_t()[source]¶

Compute shell-dependent exchange amplitudes t_n.

compute_eta()[source]¶

Compute partial exchange corrections for s and p channels.

compute_eta_total()[source]¶

Compute total exchange correction and interpolation function.

Mathematical helpers for relativistic wavefunctions and radiative terms.

spades.math_stuff.hydrogenic_binding_energy(z, n, k)[source]¶

Approximate Dirac hydrogenic binding energy for a (n, kappa) shell.

Parameters:

  • z (int) –

  • n (int) –

  • k (int) –

spades.math_stuff.dirac_gamma(kappa, z)[source]¶

Return the relativistic angular factor sqrt(kappa^2 - (alpha Z)^2).

Parameters:

  • kappa (float) –

  • z (float) –

spades.math_stuff.dirac_nu(energy, z, kappa)[source]¶

Return the Dirac phase contribution used in Coulomb phase shifts.

Parameters:

  • energy (float) –

  • z (float) –

  • kappa (int) –

spades.math_stuff.sommerfeld_param(z1, z2, energy)[source]¶

Return Sommerfeld parameter for two charges at electron kinetic energy energy.

spades.math_stuff.coulomb_phase_shift(energy, z_inf, kappa)[source]¶

Compute asymptotic Coulomb phase shift for relativistic partial wave kappa.

Parameters:

  • energy (float) –

  • z_inf (float) –

  • kappa (int) –

spades.math_stuff.r_radiative(e_total, e_max)[source]¶

Compute Sirlin-like radiative correction factor R for beta spectra.

Serialization helpers for wavefunctions, spectra, PSFs, and Fermi functions.

spades.io_handler.write_scattring_wf(file_name, kappa, ke_grid, inner_grid, coulomb_grid, r_values, p, q)[source]¶

Write scattering wavefunctions to a compact binary format.

Parameters:

  • file_name – Output binary file path.

  • kappa (list[int]) – List of relativistic angular quantum numbers.

  • ke_grid (ndarray) – Kinetic-energy grid.

  • inner_grid (dict) – Dictionaries of inner and Coulomb phase shifts keyed by kappa.

  • coulomb_grid (dict) – Dictionaries of inner and Coulomb phase shifts keyed by kappa.

  • r_values (ndarray) – Radial grid values.

  • p (dict) – Dictionaries containing large/small wavefunction components by kappa and energy index.

  • q (dict) – Dictionaries containing large/small wavefunction components by kappa and energy index.

spades.io_handler.read_scattering_wf(file_name)[source]¶

Read scattering wavefunctions from the binary format written by write_scattring_wf.

Parameters:

file_name – Input binary file path.

Returns:

(k_values, e_values, r_values, inner_phase_values, coulomb_values, p_values, q_values).

Return type:

tuple

spades.io_handler.write_bound_wf(file_name, r_grid, be_values, p_values, q_values)[source]¶

Write bound-state radial functions and binding energies to binary.

Parameters:

  • file_name – Output binary file path.

  • r_grid (ndarray) – Radial grid.

  • be_values (dict) – Nested dictionary of binding energies by principal quantum number and kappa.

  • p_values (dict) – Nested dictionaries of large/small components sampled on r_grid.

  • q_values (dict) – Nested dictionaries of large/small components sampled on r_grid.

spades.io_handler.read_bound_wf(file_name)[source]¶

Read bound-state binary data written by write_bound_wf().

Parameters:

file_name – Input binary file path.

Returns:

(r_grid, be_values, p_values, q_values).

Return type:

tuple

spades.io_handler.write_spectra(file_name, parent_nucleus, process, e_grid, spectra, psfs=None)[source]¶

Write 1D spectra and optional PSFs in a human-readable text format.

Parameters:

  • file_name – Output text file path.

  • parent_nucleus (str) – Parent nucleus label.

  • process (str) – Process identifier string.

  • e_grid (ndarray) – 1D kinetic-energy grid.

  • spectra (dict) – Nested spectra dictionary keyed by Fermi-function and spectrum names.

  • psfs (dict | None) – Optional nested PSF dictionary.

spades.io_handler.load_data(filename)[source]¶

Load spectra/PSF text files written by write_spectra().

Parameters:

filename – Input text file path.

Returns:

Parsed metadata, energy grid, spectra, and optional PSFs.

Return type:

dict

spades.io_handler.write_2d_spectra(file_name, parent_nucleus, process, e1_grid, e2_grid, emin, spectra, psfs)[source]¶

Write 2D spectra and PSFs on transformed (e1, e2) grids.

Parameters:

  • file_name – Output text file path.

  • parent_nucleus (str) – Parent nucleus label.

  • process (str) – Process identifier string.

  • e1_grid (ndarray) – 2D meshgrids used for spectra sampling.

  • e2_grid (ndarray) – 2D meshgrids used for spectra sampling.

  • emin (float) – Lower kinetic-energy threshold used by the transformation.

  • spectra (dict) – Nested dictionaries with spectra arrays and PSF values.

  • psfs (dict) – Nested dictionaries with spectra arrays and PSF values.

spades.io_handler.load_2d_spectra(filename)[source]¶

Load 2D spectra files written by write_2d_spectra().

Parameters:

filename – Input text file path.

Returns:

Parsed metadata, 2D grids, spectra arrays, and PSFs.

Return type:

dict

spades.io_handler.write_fermi_functions(file_name, parent_nucleus, process, e_grid, ff0, ff1=None)[source]¶

Write Fermi-function tables versus kinetic energy.

Parameters:

  • file_name – Output text file path.

  • parent_nucleus (str) – Parent nucleus label.

  • process (str) – Process identifier string.

  • e_grid (ndarray) – 1D kinetic-energy grid.

  • ff0 (dict) – Fermi-function values keyed by backend type.

  • ff1 (dict | None) – Fermi-function values keyed by backend type.

spades.io_handler.load_fermi_functions(filename)[source]¶

Load Fermi-function tables written by write_fermi_functions().

Parameters:

filename – Input text file path.

Returns:

Parsed energy grid and FF datasets.

Return type:

dict
